branches.comment: Blocks only the document-CAPTURE surfaces of the alcohol-ID flow — the license-scan camera (an image of a government ID) and the signature pad/handoff. The instruction screens (the ID-check 'verify the ID matches the recipient' and the alcohol arrival card) are NOT blocked: they carry no document image and only customer name/address, which the dropoff parse hashes (we hash customers, we don't block them). See alcohol_id_check + dropoff_pre_arrival.

screens.comment: Two dropoff layouts (#462/#460): the en-route nav card ('Deliver to <name>' + Directions/Call/Message) and the arrival detail card ('Delivery for' label with the name in a sibling node + 'Hand it to recipient'). The arrival card was dropping to UNKNOWN — its first text is 'Delivery for'/'Deliver by', neither of which startsWith 'Deliver to'. 'Hand it to recipient' is the dropoff-specific discriminator (the pickup card also carries 'Delivery for', so keying on that alone would steal it). The alcohol variant of the arrival card additionally carries the priority-0 sensitive id/signature banner (#463), so it is blocked before reaching here.

allTextContainsAll: pick up each one to continue
pick up each one to continue

screens.comment: Repositioning-to-zone while online (idle, no offer yet). The id form is the bottom-sheet variant; the text form (#462) is the full-screen 'Navigate to zone / We'll look for orders along the way / Spot saved until …' card, which ships no viewIds.

exists.hasTextContaining: We'll look for orders along the way
look for orders along the way

any.hasTextContaining: ask the customer for the unique

exists.hasTextContaining: what pickup issues can we help with

screens.comment: #462 batch 1 — recognize-only interstitials/menus/surveys so they no longer fall to UNKNOWN. No state.flow (cannot perturb the machine) and no parse (no PII). Keyed on 2+ stable non-PII strings.

exists.hasTextContaining: Order still being prepared when I arrived

screens.comment: Deeper than pickup_select_issue — the resolution panel reached after picking an issue. Keyed on its own distinctive strings so it never overlaps the issue list.

any.hasTextContaining: How safe did you feel on this delivery

screens.comment: Alcohol ID-verify instruction CHECKLIST — no recipient PII, no capture surface, so it is recognized as a flow step (deliberately distinct from the priority-0 sensitive id-verification capture screens, #463).

exists.hasTextContaining: Do NOT hand over items until ID has been verified

screens.comment: Alcohol verification complete (step 4 of 4) — non-sensitive completion prompt.

Alcohol ID-CHECK instruction screen (step 2 of 4) — the dasher visually verifies the recipient's ID matches and they aren't intoxicated. Instruction text only (NO recipient PII, NO document image), so it is recognized as a flow step, not blocked. Keyed on the recipient-specific text so it can't match a dasher's own identity screen (#463 reversal).

exists.hasTextContaining: You're all set to receive offers

screens.comment: #462 batch 2 — recognize-only idle/lifecycle + pickup/dropoff dialogs that shipped no viewIds and fell to UNKNOWN. No state.flow, no parse.

exists.hasTextContaining: Confirm that the code was scanned

require.anyFieldMatchesRegex: added \$(\d+\.\d{2}) tip on a past (.+?) order delivered at (\d{1,2}/\d{1,2}, \d{1,2}:\d{2} [AP]M)

all.titleContains: Confirm you have the right order
